info.name: VC xAPI — Configurable Products (GraphQL)
info.description:
GraphQL xAPI configurable products collection. Mirrors `regression/suites/Backend/graphql/050i-graphql-configurations.csv` (32 CFG-GQL-* cases) and reuses the canonical fixtures from `test-data/graphql/`.

**Note on layout** — Postman MCP `createCollection` does not accept nested folders, so requests are flattened with a numeric prefix (`00 …`, `01 …`, etc). Drag them into folders inside Postman UI if you want; Collection Runner is order-preserving regardless.

**Sections**
- 00-Auth — storefront-user OAuth2 token (stored on environment as authToken).
- 01-Queries — productConfiguration variants + configurationItems read-back. CFG-GQL-001..005.
- 02-Preview — createConfiguredLineItem (does NOT mutate cart). CFG-GQL-006..007.
- 03-Cart Setup — addItem with both required sections (captures cartId + lineItemId).
- 04-Section CRUD — add/update/remove single+bulk + changeCartConfiguredItem. CFG-GQL-008..012.
- 05-Selection (PR #114) — changeCartConfigurationItemSelected, select/unSelect [batch] + select/unSelectAll, validation + no-op contracts. CFG-GQL-013..022.
- 06-Cleanup — removeCart teardown.

**Defaults** CFG_LAPTOP (`c972b4d0-25c2-4d7c-8a18-9360a8889bc3`, CFG-013, two REQUIRED Product sections) is the default `configurableProductId`. After 01 productConfiguration runs, sectionRamId/sectionStorageId/optRamBaseId/optStorageBaseId etc. are auto-captured. After 03 addItem runs, cartId + lineItemId are captured.

**Endpoint** {{baseUrl}}/graphql
**Auth** Storefront ORG_USER via {{userEmail}}/{{userPassword}}.
**Environment** Use VCST-QA (env id `15325423-a975b69c-3ebb-430e-83c2-4da152188df6`).

**Pre-seeded CFG product GUIDs** (collection vars): `cfgHoodieId` (CFG-003 non-configurable baseline), `cfgRingId` (CFG-017 Text section maxLength=30), `cfgConditionalBikeId` (CFG-022 conditional cascade).

exec: } catch (e) { console.warn('Could not decode JWT for userId:', e); }

request.description: OAuth2 password-grant token. Default: storefront ORG_USER ({{userEmail}}/{{userPassword}}). Stores access_token on environment as authToken; collection-level Bearer picks it up. Decodes JWT sub claim into collection var userId.
Happy path: CFG_LAPTOP has 2 REQUIRED Product sections (RAM, Storage). Captures section IDs and BASE/UPGRADE option product IDs into collection variables for downstream mutations.
CFG_HOODIE is a plain Physical product. Resolver should return empty configurationSections (NOT errors[], NOT null).

exec: var hasData = d.data !== null && d.data !== undefined;

request.description: Zero-GUID. Must NOT 500. Either errors[] non-empty OR data non-null.
CFG_RING REQUIRED Text section maxLength=30 (PR #2235 server-returned client-side validation).

exec: pm.test('At least one child has non-null dependsOnSectionId', () => {
var hasChild = s.some(sec => sec.dependsOnSectionId !== null && sec.dependsOnSectionId !== undefined);
pm.test('At least one root has null dependsOnSectionId', () => {
var hasRoot = s.some(sec => sec.dependsOnSectionId === null || sec.dependsOnSectionId === undefined);

request.description: CFG_CONDITIONAL_BIKE: chain A->B->C with sibling D. Verify dependsOnSectionId for child sections.

item.name: 01 :: configurationItems — Read Back Cart Line Item Config

exec: if (items.length) console.log('Snapshot:', JSON.stringify(items.map(i => ({sectionId: i.sectionId, type: i.type, productId: i.productId, customText: i.customText, selectedForCheckout: i.selectedForCheckout}))));

request.description: Read-back companion to mutations. Requires lineItemId from 03-Cart Setup.

exec: // Note: data.id is expected to be null — preview is non-committable until persisted via addItem (matches CFG-GQL-007 contract).

request.description: Builds preview ConfigurationLineItemType with both required Product sections at BASE. Returns committable shape (id non-null). Does NOT mutate cart.

item.name: 02 :: createConfiguredLineItem — Empty Required Sections Draft id=null (CFG-GQL-007)

request.description: Soft-reject contract: empty configurationSections returns DRAFT preview with id=null (no errors).

item.name: 03 :: addItem — CFG_LAPTOP With Both Required Sections (BASE)

exec: pm.test('Cart has at least one item', () => pm.expect(d.data.addItem.itemsCount).to.be.above(0));

request.description: Precondition for 04-Section CRUD and 05-Selection. Adds CFG_LAPTOP qty=1 with both required Product sections at BASE. Captures cartId + lineItemId.

exec: pm.test('RAM section now points at UPGRADE option', () => {

request.description: Single section upsert. Replaces existing RAM option with UPGRADE; configurationItems length stays 2.
Bulk upsert variant. Length unchanged at 2; both sections now at UPGRADE.
REPLACE semantics: pass full ConfigurationSectionInput; RAM reverts to BASE.
Bulk REPLACE. Length unchanged at 2; both sections at UPGRADE.

request.description: Removes RAM section by (sectionId, type). Storage remains; configurationItems length=1.

exec: pm.test('No GraphQL errors (idempotent — RAM may already be gone)', () => pm.expect(d.errors).to.be.undefined);
// Note: read-back via configurationItems query is source of truth; immediate response may carry stale items[].configurationItems.

request.description: Bulk remove (idempotent — RAM may already be gone). configurationItems empty after.
Atomic re-configure: change quantity AND replace full configuration in one mutation.
Single-item selection toggle. Reprice triggers ONLY when flag actually flips (NCS short-circuit). Captures deflatedListPrice for follow-up CFG-GQL-014.

exec: pm.test('listPrice INCREASED back from deflated baseline', () => pm.expect(li.listPrice.amount).to.be.above(deflated - 0.001));

request.description: Round-trip parity: reprice up after CFG-GQL-013 deselect.
Batch select via ConfigurationSectionKeyInput[]. Server hardcodes selectedForCheckout=true.
Batch unselect. Server hardcodes selectedForCheckout=false.
Lineitem-scoped — flips ALL config items on the lineItemId to true.
Lineitem-scoped — flips ALL config items on the lineItemId to false.

exec: pm.test('Validation rejected: errors[] non-empty OR data is null', () => {
pm.expect(hasErrors || dataNull, 'expected errors[] non-empty OR data null — found: errors=' + JSON.stringify(d.errors) + ' data=' + JSON.stringify(d.data)).to.be.true;

request.description: Negative test: empty lineItemId must produce structured GraphQL errors[] (NOT HTTP 500).
Per PROPOSED-BL-CART-CFG-003: an unknown sectionId is a silent no-op.
Teardown: removes the cart by id (no-op if cart already gone).

variable.description: Default: CFG_LAPTOP (CFG-013) two REQUIRED Product sections RAM+Storage.
